evals.prompt: Plan Sprint 7 for acme/widgets, project #4. We have a 4-person team on a two-week cadence.
evals.expected_output: Reads current board state and open milestones, proposes a candidate issue set sized to team capacity, asks for confirmation before writing, then sets the Sprint iteration field on the confirmed items.

deterministic_checks.description: Reads current board state before proposing anything
Asks for confirmation before writing field values
The final report references Story Points against capacity

expectations: Calls get_project_items and list_milestones before proposing anything
Presents a candidate sprint roster and asks for confirmation before calling set_field_value on any item
Only calls set_field_value after the user confirms the proposed roster
The final report lists each selected item's Status, Story Points, and the total committed points against capacity

evals.prompt: A sprint-planning-request.yml issue was just submitted for acme/widgets — project #4, sprint 'Sprint 8', team size 5, cadence 2 weeks. Handle it.
evals.expected_output: Reads the submitted issue form via gh issue view instead of asking the user to repeat the team size/cadence, then proceeds with the same propose-then-confirm flow.

deterministic_checks.description: Reads the submitted form via gh issue view rather than asking the user to restate it

expectations: Reads the submitted form's rendered fields rather than asking the user to restate team size and cadence
Still proposes the sprint roster for confirmation before writing any field value

evals.prompt: Fill out the next sprint for acme/widgets, project #4, but skip anything tagged needs-design.
evals.expected_output: The proposed sprint roster excludes items tagged needs-design, and the exclusion is stated explicitly in the proposal.

deterministic_checks.description: Explicitly names the excluded tag in the proposal

expectations: The proposed candidate set does not include any item the user said to skip
The proposal explicitly states which items were excluded and why
